titleStmt.title: Letter from May O'Callaghan to Francis Sheehy Skeffington, 18 November 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: May O'Callaghan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

msIdentifier.collection: Sheehy Skeffington Papers, Collection List No. 47; Ms 33,604/13-41

correspAction.placeName: 400 Old Ford Road, Bow, London, England

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
400 OLD FORD ROAD, BOW, E. 18.xi.15 Dear Mrs. Skeffington We are arranging
for our Xmas numbers which will
be dated December 18th and we
thought it would be nice to
have something in the way of a
story or article from a real Irish
thinker — meaning you — Do you think you could find
time? i was saying to Miss Pankhurst
how nice it would be to be
more in touch with the Irish Citizen,
and I feel sure - as she likes
your paper too - that should you
ever want her to write you
anything she would try to find time
(She does not know I am writing this)
would you care to exchange
adverts of our papers? I
think it would be mutual help Hoping you will let me know
at your earliest convenience whether
we may accept a literary
contribution.
Thanking you in advance.
Yours sincerely May O'Callaghan
